note: Fictional strategist fix set for the public showcase (v2.6). All targets, outlets, and numbers are invented. Competitors named are real public companies used only as market context; URLs are illustrative and do not resolve.
summary: Northwind vets cleanly by name but is absent from the category answer. Win the broken job by getting into the roundups AI already cites, shipping the comparison and original-data assets that give models a Northwind source to pull, and converting milestone press into sustained category coverage. Protect the strong assessment job with low-effort polish only.

fixes.title: Earn placement in the category roundups AI already cites
fixes.why: The single highest-leverage move on the broken job. Northwind is absent from every roundup the measured answers cite, and Perplexity (the most roundup-dependent surface) never names it. These pages, not the homepage, decide the category shortlist.
fixes.how: Brief the authors of the four payout-API roundups the answers cite (finextra 'top stablecoin payment infrastructure providers', stablecoininsider 'stablecoin payment providers in 2026', the cobo 2026 guide, and startupstash's stablecoin-infrastructure list), armed with the 2026 payout benchmark data, and earn inclusion in each refresh.
fixes.metric_moved: discoverability mention rate (33% today), share of voice
fixes.constraint_check: Earned placement via author outreach with real data, not paid listings or manufactured engagement. No reddit-seeding.

fixes.title: Ship honest comparison pages against the rivals winning the citations
fixes.why: Rivals' own product pages won the category citations (Bridge cited in 6 of 9 discovery answers); Northwind has zero comparison or alternatives pages, so there is nothing on-domain for a model to pull when a buyer weighs options.
fixes.how: Publish current, honest comparison pages for Circle, Bridge, and Stripe payouts, the three rivals whose own pages win the category answers today, framed around the single-API full-flow (pay-in, convert, payout) differentiator and the PSP/fintech ICP.
fixes.metric_moved: discoverability citation rate (11%), own-domain share of voice
fixes.constraint_check: Additive, honest comparisons against the consideration-set rivals; no competitor disparagement. Folds in the missing category-guide coverage.

fixes.rank_reason: Promoted above wikipedia_wikidata (base priority 10.2): the encyclopedic slot is gated by notability at Series B and is not realistically winnable this cycle, whereas an exclusive data story is.
fixes.title: Turn the benchmark into an exclusive data story for a trade outlet
fixes.why: Press today is milestone-only (the Series B cycle) and Northwind is rarely quoted as a source. Sustained category coverage is what builds authority for 'who leads the category' rather than 'who got funded'.
fixes.how: Package the payout benchmark as an exclusive data story for one fintech trade outlet (finextra or PYMNTS), replacing wire echoes with original reporting that frames Northwind as the category data source.
fixes.metric_moved: discoverability mention rate, press citation share
fixes.constraint_check: Earned editorial via a data exclusive, not syndication. Depends on the benchmark being published first.

fixes.title: Publish a recurring, ungated Stablecoin Payouts Index
fixes.why: The citeable raw material (payout volume by corridor, settlement time, conversion spreads) already exists internally for a multi-billion-dollar run-rate, and none of it is published. A standing data asset is the strongest citation magnet for the category and the input the press and roundup fixes both lean on.
fixes.how: Ship a quarterly 'Stablecoin Payouts Index' as an ungated, HTML-first report on a real /insights hub with Dataset schema and a key-statistics block, so models have a Northwind source to cite instead of competitor and roundup domains.
fixes.constraint_check: Original-data product, the strategist's call over resurfacing the existing one-off PDF, because the gap is a recurring citeable asset, not a buried one. Anchors the press and roundup fixes.

fixes.title: Stand up the missing third-party review profiles
fixes.why: There is no G2, Capterra, or TrustRadius profile, so there is no structured peer-review signal a model can cite, while Bridge and Fireblocks carry populated categories. A low-effort surface that creates a citeable signal where there is none.
fixes.how: Claim and populate a G2 and Capterra profile in the stablecoin-payments category with a handful of genuine customer reviews from named customers (Latitude Payments, Harborline, Tropic Remit).
fixes.constraint_check: Real reviews solicited from real customers, never stuffed or incentivized.

fixes.title: Close the by-name FAQ and pricing gaps (protect, do not over-invest)
fixes.why: Assessment is already strong; this is protective polish. The FAQ misses the pricing and typical-customer questions buyers ask by name, and pricing routes entirely to 'Talk to sales', so by-name answers cannot cover cost.
fixes.how: Add 'How does Northwind Pay pricing work?' and 'Who are typical Northwind Pay customers?' to the FAQ with FAQPage markup, and publish a directional pricing-model page (per-transaction payout fee, FX spread, developer tier with no minimum).
fixes.constraint_check: Low-effort assessment polish; deliberately ranked below all discoverability work per the brief's protect-do-not-over-invest guardrail.

notes: wikipedia_wikidata ranks third on raw priority (P10.2) but is a known Series-B notability ceiling, so it is held off the fix set as a watch item rather than a near-term lever. search_index_presence is indeterminate (Bing returned a challenge page); confirm index presence before prescribing any indexation work.
